checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains text that is directly mapped from the source PDF. No additional or invented sentences or sections were found.
All contact information provided in the output, including the URL (www.pencweb.org), email address (Carol@pencweb.org), and phone number (919.788.9299), perfectly matches the source document.

The document consists of two pages. Page 1 contains the header and main body text. Page 2 contains the conclusion, signature, and contact information. The output JSON includes all these elements across the sections and elements arrays, representing the full document.
There are no tables in the source document.

checks.a_analysis: The text within each paragraph in the JSON output corresponds exactly to the content in the source PDF. No significant portions of text or key information have been omitted.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting

Minor typos/grammatical errors present in the source document (e.g., 'ensure financially stability' in the 2nd paragraph and 'opportunity weigh in' in the 6th paragraph) were faithfully preserved in the output text.

checks.a_analysis: Each paragraph and image from the two-page source document appears exactly once in the output JSON. There are no instances of repeated blocks or content appearing in multiple formats.

The source document is a standard professional letter and does not contain traditional structural headings (like H1, H2). The 'Subject' line is bolded in the source and is correctly identified as the document title in the metadata. The JSON renders the content as a flat list of paragraphs and images, which is appropriate for this document type and does not violate heading hierarchy rules.
The source document does not contain any tables.

The output JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right reading order of the source letter. It correctly sequences the logo, header information, salutation, body text (transitioning correctly between page 1 and page 2), closing, signature, and contact information block.
The source document does not contain any lists.

The links for the organization's website (www.pencweb.org) and the contact email (Carol@pencweb.org) found in the footer of the source are correctly identified and tagged in the final paragraph element of the JSON.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the information in the HTML output largely follows the source PDF. The header info (To, From, Date, Subject) and paragraphs are placed in the same relative sequence. While there is a duplication of content (addressed in V4), the basic single-column layout effectively translates the source document's structure into an accessible web format.
The source PDF document consists of text paragraphs and header metadata only; it does not contain any tables. Therefore, there are no tables to evaluate for rendering issues.

The source PDF on page 2 includes a handwritten signature image for 'Carol Vandenbergh' above the typed name. This handwritten signature is missing from the HTML output, which only displays the typed name and title. In the context of a signed formal letter, the handwritten signature is a meaningful visual element for authentication and its omission is a defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: Handwritten signature image for Carol Vandenbergh on page 2 of the PDF.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows only the typed text 'Carol Vandenbergh, J.D.' with no corresponding signature image.
checks.b_evidence_location: Signature block at the end of the letter on PDF page 2.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output contains a major content duplication error. The text content from the first page of the PDF (from 'To: Members...' through '...proposal does not') is rendered once, and then the entire document text (starting again from 'To: Members...') is rendered immediately following. This is a duplication of the actual body content of the document, not a navigational or structural element like a Table of Contents.
checks.b_evidence_source: The text of the letter starting from 'To: Members of the NC State Health Plan Board of Trustees' through '...proposal does not' (found on PDF page 1).
checks.b_evidence_output: The text block from 'To: Members of the NC State Health Plan Board of Trustees' through '...proposal does not' appears twice in the HTML rendering.
checks.b_evidence_location: In the HTML output, the content repeats midway through the document.
